metadata.source_root_policy: Paths are relative to the MathDevMCP checkout root. Sibling repositories use ../ segments and are local-only.
metadata.scoring_policy: Cases are scored by declared evidence dimensions and boundary checks. No aggregate accuracy or public benchmark gate is emitted.

refuted.scoring_semantics: Counts only when a scoped backend, local schema adapter, or concrete counterexample supplies the contradiction; prose alone is insufficient.

missing_assumptions.scoring_semantics: Counts as a good outcome when the workflow identifies absent source- or route-required assumptions without inventing them.

backend_unavailable.scoring_semantics: Counts as good abstention only when the intended backend route is named and unavailability is not treated as refutation.

not_encodable.scoring_semantics: Counts as good abstention only when the current grammar limitation is explicit and the claim is not declared false.

insufficient_evidence.scoring_semantics: Counts as good abstention when source/backend evidence is insufficient and the smallest next evidence action is named.

routing_only.scoring_semantics: Counts only for route-selection diagnostics; it is not mathematical correctness evidence.

derive_from.comparator: Phase 1 inventory route and existing derive_from envelope
derive_from.primary_criterion: Return a scoped derivation, missing-assumption result, or justified abstention with source anchors, explicit givens/assumptions, backend route, and non-claims.
veto_diagnostics: LLM prose treated as derivation proof
inconclusive route treated as source failure
derive_from.good_abstention: Name the missing assumption, unavailable backend, or unsupported source rule and preserve the non-claim boundary.
derive_from.result_artifact: Phase 4/5 per-case review packet plus route-availability ledger row.
forbidden_claims: full source derivation proved without backend/source adapter
scientific or empirical validity follows from derivation route

prove_or_counterexample.comparator: Phase 1 inventory route and existing prove_or_counterexample envelope
prove_or_counterexample.primary_criterion: Return a scoped proof, concrete counterexample/refutation, or justified abstention with backend/source distinction and non-claims.
veto_diagnostics: formal-proof claim made without formal backend certificate
prove_or_counterexample.good_abstention: State that the route is not encodable, backend unavailable, or evidence insufficient without saying the claim is false.
prove_or_counterexample.result_artifact: Phase 4/5 per-case review packet plus route-availability ledger row.
forbidden_claims: production correctness from scoped proof result

assumptions_for.comparator: Phase 1 inventory route and existing assumptions_for envelope
assumptions_for.primary_criterion: List route-required assumptions with source/backend provenance, status, and minimality non-claim.
assumptions_for.good_abstention: Declare missing assumptions and the smallest source/backend evidence needed to resolve them.
assumptions_for.result_artifact: Phase 4/5 per-case review packet plus route-availability ledger row.
forbidden_claims: downstream sampler or implementation validity follows

debug_derivation.comparator: Phase 1 inventory route and existing debug_derivation/proof-gap envelope
debug_derivation.primary_criterion: Localize the first unsupported, refuted, or source-mismatched step with source/backend evidence and local-gap non-claims.
veto_diagnostics: localized gap treated as global theorem failure
probe refutation used to invalidate unrelated source conclusions
debug_derivation.good_abstention: Return a local gap or unresolved route with human-review action rather than overclaiming source failure.
debug_derivation.result_artifact: Phase 4/5 per-case review packet plus route-availability ledger row.
forbidden_claims: scientific conclusion invalidated by local sign probe

audit_math_to_code.comparator: Phase 1 inventory route and existing audit_math_to_code envelope
audit_math_to_code.primary_criterion: Report structural match or mismatch with code/equation evidence while preserving semantic-proof non-claims.
veto_diagnostics: structural match treated as semantic proof
missing literal term treated as complete mathematical invalidity
audit_math_to_code.good_abstention: Name missing terms or alias ambiguity and route to human review without proving code correctness or incorrectness.
audit_math_to_code.result_artifact: Phase 4/5 per-case review packet plus route-availability ledger row.
forbidden_claims: code is mathematically wrong from structural mismatch alone

prepare_review_packet.comparator: Phase 1 inventory route and existing prepare_review_packet envelope
prepare_review_packet.primary_criterion: Emit a durable diagnostic packet with sources, assumptions, backend attempts, gaps, actions, evidence classes, and non-claims.
veto_diagnostics: review packet treated as proof certificate
prepare_review_packet.good_abstention: Make diagnostic-only status explicit and identify unresolved evidence without certifying the claim.
prepare_review_packet.result_artifact: Phase 4/5 per-case review packet plus route-availability ledger row.
forbidden_claims: full derivation proved by packet assembly

non_claims: This manifest is not public benchmark evidence.
This manifest is not benchmark-gate evidence.
This manifest is not release-readiness evidence.
This manifest does not establish scientific validity, production correctness, external reproducibility, full LaTeX proof checking, or broad theorem proving.

cases.title: IFT gradient-bias sign gap
cases.question: Where does this sign derivation first fail?

human_framing.case_purpose: Test whether the workflow can localize a sign inconsistency in an IFT-style derivation without turning a local algebraic failure into a global criticism of the source document.
human_framing.local_background: In a derivation that tracks a gradient or bias term, changing a sign can reverse the interpretation of a local comparative-static effect. The packet only checks one localized transition, so the reviewer should treat it as a focused debugging artifact rather than a statement about the whole DSGE note.
human_framing.minimal_formula_scaffold: The localized obligation is -lam*dr = lam*dr. Without additional assumptions forcing lam*dr = 0, these expressions differ by -2*lam*dr.
human_framing.source_context_summary: The cited source regions contain the theorem/proof algebra and a later summary of the sign convention. They motivate why a sign-consistency check is meaningful, but the packet does not copy or certify the whole derivation.
human_framing.decision_target: Decide whether the encoded transition -lam*dr = lam*dr is valid under the stated assumptions.
decision_criteria: Refute if a concrete assignment satisfying the stated assumptions makes the two sides differ.
Prove only if a backend certifies the encoded equality under the stated assumptions.
Abstain if notation binding, source translation, or assumptions are insufficient.
alternative_explanations: The source may use a sign convention or preceding definition not represented in the encoded obligation.
The adapter may have localized the wrong transition.
Additional assumptions could make lam*dr vanish, but none are supplied here.
what_would_change_conclusion: A source-backed sign convention showing the encoded obligation is not the intended transition.
An explicit assumption lam*dr = 0 or equivalent degeneracy.
A corrected adapter mapping that produces a different obligation.

scoring_rubric.primary_pass_semantics: Localize the first sign inconsistency or explicitly preserve unresolved source-adapter gaps without global document claims.

negative_control.scoring_semantics: A bounded sign-flip refutation may score only with a concrete counterexample or source-adapter contradiction and local-gap non-claims.

cases.good_abstention: If the source adapter cannot bind notation, return gap_found or insufficient evidence with line anchors and human-review action.

cases.title: Kalman prediction-error log-likelihood assumptions
cases.question: What assumptions are required to derive Kalman prediction-error log-likelihood?

human_framing.case_purpose: Test whether the workflow can identify route-required assumptions for the Gaussian Kalman prediction-error likelihood instead of silently treating the likelihood expression as well posed.
human_framing.local_background: A Gaussian prediction-error likelihood scores forecast errors using their covariance. The log determinant term measures uncertainty volume, and the quadratic solve term measures the innovation size after scaling by the innovation covariance.
human_framing.minimal_formula_scaffold: For innovation v and innovation covariance S, the local expression is logdet(S) + v.T @ solve(S, v), representing log det(S) plus v' S^{-1} v.
human_framing.source_context_summary: The cited Kalman-filter sources describe the prediction-error decomposition, innovation regularity, and the likelihood spine. The packet summarizes those roles rather than quoting raw source excerpts.
human_framing.decision_target: Decide which assumptions must be explicit before treating logdet(S) + v.T @ solve(S, v) as a well-posed route target.
decision_criteria: List missing route-required assumptions such as square/invertible covariance, valid logdet domain, positive-definiteness when needed, and conformable dimensions.
Do not claim the list is globally minimal.
Do not treat the assumption list as a proof of implementation or sampler correctness.
alternative_explanations: Some assumptions may be stated elsewhere in the source but not supplied to this route.
A different likelihood convention may alter constants or signs without changing the domain requirements.
Masking or partially observed components may require additional source-specific assumptions.
what_would_change_conclusion: Supplying source-backed positive-definiteness/invertibility and dimension assumptions.
Providing a source adapter that binds observed-component and mask conventions.
Supplying implementation evidence only if the question changes from assumptions to code correctness.

scoring_rubric.primary_pass_semantics: Identify linear-Gaussian, innovation covariance, observed-component/mask, logdet, solve, and conformability assumptions without claiming implementation proof.

cases.good_abstention: If assumptions are incomplete, return missing_assumptions with source anchors and no likelihood-proof claim.

cases.title: Joseph covariance update equivalence
cases.question: Can we prove Joseph and compact covariance updates are equivalent in scope?

human_framing.case_purpose: Test whether the workflow can prove a small scoped algebraic equivalence in Kalman covariance updates while preserving the boundary between algebraic equality and numerical stability.
human_framing.local_background: Kalman filtering can write covariance updates in compact or Joseph-like forms. Algebraic equality in exact arithmetic does not guarantee equal floating-point stability, so the packet must keep those claims separate.
human_framing.minimal_formula_scaffold: The encoded scalar obligation is (1-k*h)*p = p-k*h*p. A symbolic proof checks that lhs - rhs simplifies to zero.
human_framing.source_context_summary: The cited source regions discuss compact and Joseph covariance update forms and include a numerical caveat. The packet uses that context only to justify the scoped algebraic obligation.
human_framing.decision_target: Decide whether the encoded scalar compact/Joseph update equality is algebraically valid.
decision_criteria: Prove if a symbolic backend certifies lhs - rhs = 0.
Refute if a concrete assignment makes the encoded sides differ.
Abstain if source translation, dimensions, or assumptions are not clear enough.
alternative_explanations: The scalar encoding may omit matrix assumptions needed for the full covariance statement.
Floating-point implementations may behave differently even when exact algebra matches.
A backend certificate for this obligation does not validate production code.
what_would_change_conclusion: A source review showing the scalar obligation is not the intended scoped claim.
Additional matrix-domain requirements that invalidate the scalar reduction.
A separate numerical-stability or code-validation study for broader claims.

source_files.role: compact and Joseph covariance update forms

scoring_rubric.primary_pass_semantics: Support only exact-arithmetic algebraic equivalence with Kalman-gain assumptions and numerical caveat.

cases.good_abstention: If matrix assumptions are unavailable, state insufficient evidence instead of proving floating-point stability.

cases.title: Affine pricing recursion
cases.question: Can we derive affine pricing recursion from Gaussian affine assumptions?

human_framing.case_purpose: Test whether the workflow can route an affine pricing-recursion derivation and abstain when the current scalar symbolic route is too weak for matrix/domain reasoning.
human_framing.local_background: Affine pricing recursions typically use an exponential-affine ansatz and Gaussian conditional moments to collect constant and state-linear coefficients. The useful review question is whether the route has enough assumptions and algebraic structure to derive those recursions.
human_framing.minimal_formula_scaffold: A full recursion usually needs an affine law of motion, a Gaussian moment-generating step, and coefficient collection. The current baseline obligation A + B*x = A + x*B is only a small commutation/collection proxy.
human_framing.source_context_summary: The cited source region contains the general discrete-time affine pricing recursion. The packet checks whether the current route can support a scoped derivation without claiming empirical pricing validity.
human_framing.decision_target: Decide whether the current encoded route can derive the affine recursion from the supplied givens and assumptions.
decision_criteria: Prove or derive only if the route has the required Gaussian MGF, affine-state, and coefficient-collection assumptions.
Abstain if matrix/domain review is required before scalar symbolic routing.
Do not use a small algebraic proxy as evidence for the full pricing recursion.
alternative_explanations: The recursion may be valid in the source but not encoded richly enough here.
Matrix dimensions or noncommutativity may matter for coefficient collection.
A source adapter may need to expose the Gaussian MGF step before backend checking is meaningful.
what_would_change_conclusion: A source-backed affine-state and Gaussian-MGF adapter.
Explicit matrix/vector dimension and commutation assumptions.
A backend route that can certify the actual coefficient recursions rather than a scalar proxy.

scoring_rubric.primary_pass_semantics: Derive or route the exponential-affine ansatz, Gaussian MGF, coefficient collection, and recurrence assumptions without empirical-pricing claims.

cases.good_abstention: If Gaussian MGF or vector/matrix collection is not available, return insufficient evidence with exact missing route.

cases.title: Kalman score same-scalar assumptions
cases.question: What assumptions are needed for the Kalman score to match the same scalar likelihood?

human_framing.case_purpose: Test whether the workflow can identify the assumptions needed for a Kalman score expression to differentiate the same scalar likelihood that the value route uses.
human_framing.local_background: A score is a derivative of the likelihood with respect to parameters. For a Kalman score to match a scalar likelihood, the value expression, solve/logdet conventions, differentiability conditions, and observed-component treatment must be the same.
human_framing.minimal_formula_scaffold: The target is grad(logdet(S) + v.T @ solve(S, v)). This requires the likelihood expression to be well posed and differentiable on the stated domain.
human_framing.source_context_summary: The cited BayesFilter sources discuss first-order Kalman score terms and the same-scalar likelihood contract. The packet asks for assumptions, not a proof of HMC readiness.
human_framing.decision_target: Decide which assumptions are missing before the Kalman score can be treated as differentiating the same scalar likelihood.
decision_criteria: List route-required assumptions for solve/logdet domain, differentiability, conformability, and same-scalar binding.
Do not claim the assumptions are globally minimal.
Do not infer posterior, Hessian, or HMC validity from the assumption list.
alternative_explanations: The source may state same-scalar conditions outside the cited route.
A different parameterization may require extra differentiability or masking assumptions.
The score formula may be algebraically correct while still not validating sampler behavior.
what_would_change_conclusion: Source-backed same-scalar, differentiability, and covariance-domain assumptions.
A source adapter that binds value and score notation to the same likelihood object.
A separate backend derivation of the score under the supplied assumptions.

source_files.role: first-order Kalman score and solve-form score

scoring_rubric.primary_pass_semantics: Identify differentiability, solve/invertibility, conformability, and same-scalar assumptions without HMC validity claims.

cases.good_abstention: Return missing_assumptions for absent same-scalar, differentiability, or solve conditions rather than proving sampler validity.

cases.title: State-space code missing solve
cases.question: Does code implement the documented Kalman likelihood solve/quadratic form?

human_framing.case_purpose: Test whether the workflow can audit code against a documented Kalman likelihood formula and report a structural mismatch without claiming full semantic incorrectness.
human_framing.local_background: In a Gaussian prediction-error likelihood, each observation contributes a log determinant term for the innovation covariance and a quadratic penalty for the innovation scaled by that covariance. A log determinant alone does not account for the size of the forecast error.
human_framing.minimal_formula_scaffold: For innovation v and innovation covariance S, the usual local contribution is log det(S) + v' S^{-1} v. In code, v' S^{-1} v commonly appears as v.T @ solve(S, v), a Cholesky solve, triangular solve, or equivalent whitening transform.
human_framing.source_context_summary: The documentation fixture states the intended solve/quadratic likelihood structure, while the candidate code fixture contains nearby state-space and slogdet machinery. The packet checks structural visibility under the configured aliases.
human_framing.decision_target: Decide whether the candidate code structurally contains both the documented logdet part and the solve/quadratic-form part.
decision_criteria: Report structural match if required documented terms and accepted aliases/decompositions appear.
Report structural mismatch if a required term is absent and no accepted equivalent is registered.
Do not infer complete mathematical correctness or incorrectness from structural matching alone.
alternative_explanations: The quadratic term may be implemented through an unregistered Cholesky, triangular solve, whitening transform, or helper function.
The documented expression may be a schematic target rather than literal code structure.
The matcher may miss semantically equivalent code when aliases or decompositions are incomplete.
what_would_change_conclusion: A registered alias or decomposition showing solve(S, innovation) is implemented equivalently.
Code-path evidence exposing the quadratic form through a helper or transformed variable.
A source correction showing the documented target should not require the solve/quadratic term.

source_files.role: candidate implementation missing explicit solve/quadratic form

scoring_rubric.primary_pass_semantics: Report structural missing solve/quadratic evidence without claiming full mathematical invalidity.

negative_control.scoring_semantics: Structural mismatch is diagnostic only and cannot certify semantic incorrectness.

cases.good_abstention: If aliases or implementation details obscure the route, return structural_mismatch or human review with semantic-proof non-claims.
forbidden_claims: Absence of literal solve proves implementation invalid

cases.title: Proof-boundary review packet
cases.question: Can we produce a packet for a difficult Gaussian score derivation without overclaiming proof?

human_framing.case_purpose: Test whether the workflow can assemble a durable review packet for a difficult score derivation while clearly stating that the packet is not itself a proof certificate.
human_framing.local_background: A review packet is a container for source anchors, assumptions, backend attempts, gaps, and next actions. It helps humans or downstream agents continue safely, but assembling evidence does not create new mathematical proof.
human_framing.minimal_formula_scaffold: The nested evidence concerns a Gaussian score route around grad(logdet(S) + v.T @ solve(S, v)), whose validity depends on solve/logdet domain, differentiability, and same-scalar assumptions.
human_framing.source_context_summary: The cited release-report region gives Gaussian score review context. The packet's role is to preserve unresolved evidence and boundaries rather than certify the full derivation.
human_framing.decision_target: Decide whether a diagnostic packet can be produced without overclaiming proof.
decision_criteria: Return diagnostic_only when nested evidence remains assumption-blocked or unresolved.
Preserve nested evidence statuses rather than upgrading them.
State explicitly that review-packet assembly is not a proof certificate.
alternative_explanations: A future backend proof could certify part of the score derivation, but that is not supplied here.
A human may accept additional source assumptions after review.
The packet may be useful operationally even when it is not mathematically decisive.
what_would_change_conclusion: Backend-certified score obligations under explicit assumptions.
Source-backed assumptions resolving the nested missing-assumption evidence.
A human-reviewed derivation note that turns diagnostic actions into checked obligations.

scoring_rubric.primary_pass_semantics: Create a diagnostic packet that preserves nested evidence meanings and states it is not a certificate.

negative_control.scoring_semantics: A good answer is diagnostic-only with explicit non-claims and next evidence actions.

cases.good_abstention: Return diagnostic_only or insufficient evidence with unresolved backend/source routes and no proof-certificate claim.

cases.title: HMC value-only boundary
cases.question: Does a value-only filtering likelihood route prove HMC production readiness?

human_framing.case_purpose: Test whether the workflow refuses to infer HMC production readiness from value-only likelihood evidence.
human_framing.local_background: HMC/NUTS readiness requires more than evaluating a likelihood value. It usually depends on gradients, differentiability, numerical stability, sampler diagnostics, implementation behavior, and target-specific validation.
human_framing.minimal_formula_scaffold: The encoded obligation asks whether value_only_likelihood implies hmc_production_readiness. These are semantic placeholders, not an algebraic identity that SymPy can certify.
human_framing.source_context_summary: The cited HMC state-space source region discusses the likelihood route and value/gradient boundary. The packet uses it to preserve the distinction between value evidence and sampler-readiness evidence.
human_framing.decision_target: Decide whether value-only filtering likelihood evidence is sufficient to prove HMC production readiness.
decision_criteria: Abstain unless gradient, differentiability, implementation, and sampler-diagnostic evidence are supplied.
Do not refute HMC merely because the value-only route is insufficient.
Do not certify production readiness from a symbolic equality between semantic placeholders.
alternative_explanations: The system may be HMC-ready under additional evidence not included in this packet.
A formal source route could connect value, gradient, and sampler readiness, but it is not supplied here.
Backend simplification of opaque placeholders is not meaningful proof of product capability.
what_would_change_conclusion: Source-backed gradient and differentiability evidence.
Implementation checks showing correct gradients and numerical behavior.
Sampler diagnostics and runtime validation under a stated evidence contract.

source_files.role: HMC state-space likelihood route and value/gradient boundary

scoring_rubric.primary_pass_semantics: Reject production-readiness proof from value-only likelihood evidence and preserve backend-unavailable/insufficient-evidence boundary.

negative_control.scoring_semantics: Good outcome is justified abstention; it must not refute HMC or certify readiness.

cases.good_abstention: State value-only evidence is insufficient for HMC readiness and name gradient/sampler/backend evidence needed.

cases.title: Affine recovery assumption limit
cases.question: Can neural-solver approximation guarantees be derived from affine recovery text alone?

human_framing.case_purpose: Test whether the workflow avoids deriving a rigorous neural-solver approximation guarantee from affine recovery prose alone.
human_framing.local_background: Affine recovery text can describe structure in a pricing problem, but a uniform neural-solver approximation guarantee normally needs a separate approximation theorem, training/error assumptions, function-class conditions, and domain bounds.
human_framing.minimal_formula_scaffold: The encoded obligation asks whether affine_recovery_text equals or entails uniform_neural_solver_bound. These are semantic placeholders, so source-backed bridge assumptions are required before derivation.
human_framing.source_context_summary: The cited nonlinear-pricing source region discusses affine recovery and approximation context. The packet checks whether that text alone supplies the assumptions needed for a uniform bound.
human_framing.decision_target: Decide whether affine recovery text alone is enough to derive a rigorous neural-solver uniform approximation guarantee.
decision_criteria: Return missing_assumptions if source-backed bridge assumptions and approximation-theorem conditions are absent.
Do not treat affine recovery prose as a proof of neural solver correctness.
Do not claim pricing validity or rigorous uniform bounds without explicit evidence.
alternative_explanations: The required approximation theorem may exist elsewhere but is not supplied to this route.
Empirical approximation performance would not by itself prove a uniform bound.
A narrower local bound might be derivable under assumptions not present in the packet.
what_would_change_conclusion: A source-backed approximation theorem with domain, regularity, and error assumptions.
Explicit training/generalization or solver-error assumptions connected to the target bound.
A formal or symbolic bridge from affine recovery structure to the claimed neural-solver guarantee.

source_files.role: affine recovery and nonlinear-pricing approximation text

cases.expected_route: source assumptions plus negative overclaim check
cases.expected_outcome_type: missing_assumptions / justified abstention for uniform bound

scoring_rubric.primary_pass_semantics: Identify that affine recovery text alone does not establish a neural-solver uniform approximation theorem.

negative_control.scoring_semantics: Good outcome is missing_assumptions or insufficient evidence with no rigorous uniform-bound claim.

cases.good_abstention: Name the absent approximation theorem, training/error assumptions, and backend/source evidence required for a uniform bound.
